MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Josh Molchan, Esq. 310-881-8578 Dordick Law Corp. for Plaintiff.
Aaron Daniels, Esq. 714-384-6600 The Aguilera Law Group for Defendant Bay Cities Pving & Grading Inc.
Kevin Larson, Esq. 650-989-5400 CLAPP, MORONEY, VUCINICH, BEEMAN and SCHELEY for Defendant CCSF
Sean Nagel Esq. 805-541-1000 Diehl & Rodewald for Defendant Pavement Engineering

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received. The Court has no authority to continue a trial date, as a general rule get trial continuance first. Court comments.

ARGUED. Mr. Larson is fine with the court's tentative ruling and informs the Court there is a signed stipulation to continue trial date.
Mr. Daniels contests and presents argument; prejudice to clients, will file a stipulation on an ex parte next week.
Mr. Nagel submits to the Court's TR, apologizes to the court, client was only added two months ago and there has been no discovery done yet.
Mr. Molchan has nothing further to add.

After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (SUED ERRONEOUSLY HEREIN AS CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO; COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint.

The City and County of San Francisco's Motion for Leave to File a Cross-Complaint is GRANTED.

Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.)

Good cause appearing, the court grants CCSF leave to file the proposed cross-complaint. The court notes trial is set for 3/6/2026. Only one party, Bay Cities Paving & Grading, Inc., has expressed concern about the trial date, and that party failed to identify any specific circumstances demonstrating cognizable prejudice or bad faith. The court will not infer prejudice or bad faith. In any event, concerns regarding the trial date must be addressed to Department 206.

Moving Defendant must file its cross-complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit 1 to the Echeverria Declaration, within five days of entry of this order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
